title: The Bread He Became
subtitle: In the upper room, after the Passover lamb, Christ gives Himself in bread and wine

stillness: Be still now, and quiet your heart.
Before the words are spoken, devotion and prayer are asked of you,
and the lifting up of the mind to God.
gospel: Jesus took bread of unleavened bread,
and lifting up His eyes to the Father, He gave thanks,
and He blessed it, and broke it,
and gave it to His disciples, and said:
Take ye, all of you, and eat:
This is My body, which is given for you.
And in like manner, taking the chalice,
again giving thanks, He blessed it, and gave it to them, saying:
Take ye, and drink ye all of this:
For this is My blood of the New Testament,
which shall be shed for many
behold: how diligently, how faithfully, how devoutly the Lord did these things,
and with His own hands gave Communion to that beloved and blessed family of His.
For the disciples did not take the Body for themselves from the Lord's hand;
but He who consecrated, He Himself also ministered it.
with which He handed over and left to us His very self,
His most holy Body and Blood, truly for food and for drink.
Drawing near to death, He ate with His Apostles
not only the lamb of the figure,
but His own true Body, sacramentally.
consider: Be astonished at this love.
What was to be, on the morrow, the sacrifice pleasing to God,
the priceless price of our redemption,
He made to be our food and our sustenance for the journey.
Willing to give an end to the sacrifices of the Law, and to begin the New Testament,
He makes Himself our sacrifice;
a Priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.
If the word of Christ is of such power that it made every creature from nothing,
it is no wonder if it should change one creature into another.
That voice was spoken once, but through all the tables of the Church,
even unto this present day, it gives the sacrifice its firmness.
And He broke the bread, to show the breaking of His Body, that is, His Passion,
which would not come except by His own will and work.
Broken three times was Christ:
by the scourges before the Passion,
by the nails in the Passion,
by the lance after the Passion.
The Lord said: thou shalt not change Me into thee, as the food of thy flesh,
but thou shalt be changed into Me.
Taste and see, for the Lord is sweet:
this is the manna, holding within it every delight, the sweetness of every savour.
Keep then in your heart the price of your redemption.
prayer: Lord Jesus, who didst hand over and leave to us Thy very self,
Thy most holy Body and Blood, truly for our food and drink:
grant me, in receiving and before receiving this sacrament,
devotion and prayer, and the lifting up of my mind to Thee.
Let me receive Thee with mind and body,
and eat Thee with faith and with mouth,
and taste and see in devotion that Thou art sweet.
Change me not into Thyself as I change earthly food,
but let me be changed into Thee.
And what I cannot do sacramentally, let me do by faith,
for Thou hast said: Believe, and thou hast eaten.

latinJewel.latin: Obstupesce illam clarissimam dignationem, et dignatissimam caritatem, qua nobis tradidit et reliquit semetipsum, scilicet sacratissimum Corpus et Sanguinem suum vere in cibum et potum.
latinJewel.english: Be astonished at that most clear condescension, and that most worthy love, with which He handed over and left to us His very self, namely His most holy Body and Blood, truly for food and for drink.
